basis.bridge_note: official = E[T]/wall = E[T]*K_cal (K_cal=125.268 = official/E[T] = steps/s). The HBM ceiling replaces the deployed wall (1/K_cal = 7982.9us) with the read floor (2933.8us local x tau_lo = 3037.2us official). token-per-read = E[T]/read_floor.

ceiling.hbm_bound_ceiling_basis: ceiling = E[T] / read_floor_official = E[T]*bandwidth/(body_bytes*tau_lo); deployed official = E[T] / (1/K_cal). frac = read_floor_official / (1/K_cal). E[T]=3.844, read_floor_official=3037.204us, deployed_wall_official=7982.888us.

non_read_slack.note: kernel_addressable = draft + verify-compute-above-read (kanna #280's hideable target); host_other ~ ubel #284 decode-loop overhead. Even removing ONLY the kernel-addressable slack reaches tps_kernel_floor (> 500), so the read floor does NOT cap the verify front below 500.

gpu_readbench.note: A reduction over a 1.76 GB fp16 tensor measures achievable READ bandwidth; the analytic floor uses NOMINAL 600 GB/s (matching #278). Achievable < nominal is expected; the analytic central floor stays the imported figure.

synthesis.handoff: The HBM-bandwidth-bound TPS ceiling is 1265.6 (official), so deployed 481.53 sits at 0.380 of the read-floor ceiling and pure kernel-timing tuning is hard-capped at 1265.6 (CLEARS 500) -- confirming the verify-side front is OPEN (the int4 read is only 38% of the honest 1/K_cal=7982.9us step; #278's 'floor>normalized-step' was composition-compression, not wall read-boundedness); the only two ceiling-raising levers are E[T] >= 1.519 (ceiling-to-500; the deployed point reaches 500 at E[T] >= 3.991, fern #281) or a -153% body-read 'reduction' (NEGATIVE -> read-reduction is non-binding, the ceiling is already >500), so the path to 500 is E[T]-raise + hideable-verify-compute (kanna #280), NOT read-reduction and NOT read-floor-limited.

levers.lever_note: et_for_500_at_read_floor (CEILING-to-500) = 1.5186 is BELOW the current E[T]=3.844 -> the ceiling is already > 500 and the E[T] lever is non-binding ON THE CEILING. To move the DEPLOYED POINT to 500 needs E[T] >= 3.9914 (== fern #281's 3.991). read_reduction_pct = -153.1% is NEGATIVE -> you would need to ADD bytes to drop the ceiling to 500; the bytes-per-read lever is non-binding. The body-read-reduction lever would only matter if the deployed step were read-bound (it is 38% read); any real reduction is PPL-gated at headroom 0.0428 and must be a SEPARATE measured leg -- do NOT re-quantize here.

synthesis.verdict: HBM-BOUND CEILING IS NON-BINDING AT 500 -- THE VERIFY-SIDE FRONT IS OPEN, NOT CAPPED. The HBM-bandwidth ceiling is 1265.6 TPS (official; 1310.2 bandwidth-pure), and deployed 481.53 sits at 0.380 of it: the int4 body read (3037us official) is only 38.0% of the HONEST official per-step wall (1/K_cal = 7982.9us), NOT the dominant cost. denken #278's 'floor (2933.8) EXCEEDS the 1218.2us step' is an artifact of the ~6.5x composition COMPRESSION of the normalized step (it proves step-shaving over-credit 4.82x, NOT wall read-boundedness). Kernel timing is NOT hard-capped below 500 by the read floor: the bare-read-floor ceiling is 1265.6 (>> 500), and removing ONLY the kernel-addressable slack (verify->read floor + draft folded) reaches 747 TPS. The front is OPEN -- gated by how much of the verify's 1.69x compute-above-read is HIDEABLE (kanna #280) and by #278's 4.8x over-credit on incremental shaves (consistent with the public field grinding 484->489.63 via split-KV/fa2sw, still <500). Body-read-reduction is the WRONG lever: the ceiling is ALREADY > 500 so reducing bytes only raises a non-binding ceiling (the -153% 'reduction for 500' is the proof). The binding levers to MOVE THE DEPLOYED POINT to 500 are E[T]-raise (fern #281: E[T] >= 3.991) or closing the 62% non-read slack (kanna #280 verify hideable-compute + ubel #284 host overhead). BASELINE 481.53 untouched; analysis-only; adds 0 TPS; NOT a launch; the gate remains a MEASURED >=500 build.
